msg: assignment discards 'const' qualifier from pointer target type (CType(CPointer(CUnsigned(CLong())),true,false,false) = CType(CPointer(CUnsigned(CLong())),false,false,true))
reasoning: Same artifact as FAMILY_45. The presence condition !def(CONFIG_NUMA)&def(CONFIG_PCI)&!def(const) again requires '!def(const)'. In the !CONFIG_NUMA branch, local_cpus_show() calls cpumask_of_node() at line 135, not cpumask_of_pcibus(), so the const-qualifier alignment is even more clearly fine. The warning only arises because the tool treats 'const' as a configuration macro, which it is not in real C code. No real build configuration matches this condition. False positive.
